com.pax.poslink

Class AbstractPosLink

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.pax.poslink.AbstractPosLink
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    PosLink

    public abstract class AbstractPosLink
extends java.lang.Object
    The parent of PosLink. Handle POSLink business.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static interface  AbstractPosLink.ReportStatusListener
      Interface definition for a callback to be invoked when terminal reported integer status.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      java.lang.String appDataFolder
      In android system, it is recommend to set the appDataFolder to getApplicationContext().getFilesDir().getAbsolutePath().
      If this is not set, it will use getApplicationContext().getFilesDir().getAbsolutePath() in Android and "./" in PC Java.

      The BatchRequest object which need to be created before assign to this property.

        The PayloadRequest object which need to be created before assign to this property.

      • PaymentResponse

        public PaymentResponse PaymentResponse
        The PaymentResponse object.

        You can get it directly if the ProcessTrans resultCode is OK.Otherwise ,it is null.
        For example:
        PosLink pos = new PosLink();
        PaymentRequest pay = new PaymentRequest();
        ... //do the assignment for the object PaymentRequest properties
        pos.PaymentRequest = pay;
        ProcessTransResult c = pos.ProcessTrans();
        if(c.Code == ProcessTransResultCode.OK)
        {
        PaymentResponse r = pos.PaymentResponse;
        //System.out.println("Result Code:"+r.ResultCode+ "\nResult Text:" +r.ResultTxt);
        }
        else
        {
        //you can't assignment for the PaymentResponse object
        }

      • ManageResponse

        public ManageResponse ManageResponse
        The ManageResponse object.

        You can get it directly if the ProcessTrans resultCode is OK.Otherwise ,it is null.
        For example:
        PosLink pos = new PosLink();
        ManageRequest man = new ManageRequest();
        ... //do the assignment for the object ManageResponse properties
        pos.ManageRequest = man;
        ProcessTransResult c = pos.ProcessTrans();
        if(c.Code == ProcessTransResultCode.OK)
        {
        ManageResponse r = pos.ManageResponse;
        //System.out.println("Result Code:"+r.ResultCode+ "\nResult Text:" +r.ResultTxt);
        }
        else
        {
        //you can't assignment for the ManageResponse object
        }

      • BatchResponse

        public BatchResponse BatchResponse
        The BatchResponse object.

        You can get it directly if the ProcessTrans resultCode is OK.Otherwise ,it is null.
        For example:
        PosLink pos = new PosLink();
        BatchRequest pay = new BatchRequest();
        ... //do the assignment for the object BatchRequest properties
        pos.BatchRequest = pay;
        ProcessTransResult c = pos.ProcessTrans();
        if(c.Code == ProcessTransResultCode.OK)
        {
        BatchResponse r = pos.BatchResponse;
        //System.out.println("Result Code:"+r.ResultCode+ "\nResult Text:" +r.ResultTxt);
        }
        else
        {
        //you can't assignment for the BatchResponse object
        }

      • ReportResponse

        public ReportResponse ReportResponse
        The ReportResponse object.

        You can get it directly if the ProcessTrans resultCode is OK.Otherwise ,it is null.
        For example:
        PosLink pos = new PosLink();
        ReportRequest pay = new ReportRequest();
        ... //do the assignment for the object ReportRequest properties
        pos.ReportRequest = pay;
        ProcessTransResult c = pos.ProcessTrans();
        if(c.Code == ProcessTransResultCode.OK)
        {
        ReportResponse r = pos.ReportResponse;
        //System.out.println("Result Code:"+r.ResultCode+ "\nResult Text:" +r.ResultTxt);
        }
        else
        {
        //you can't assignment for the pos.ReportResponse object
        }

      • PayloadResponse

        public PayloadResponse PayloadResponse
        The PayloadResponse object.

        You can get it directly if the ProcessTrans resultCode is OK.Otherwise ,it is null.
        For example:
        PosLink pos = new PosLink();
        PayloadRequest request = new PayloadRequest();
        ... //do the assignment for the object ReportRequest properties
        pos.PayloadRequest = request;
        ProcessTransResult c = pos.ProcessTrans();
        if(c.Code == ProcessTransResultCode.OK)
        {
        PayloadResponse r = pos.PayloadResponse;
        //System.out.println("Result Code:"+r.ResultCode+ "\nResult Text:" +r.ResultTxt);
        }
        else
        {
        //you can't assignment for the pos.PayloadResponse object
        }

      • appDataFolder

        public java.lang.String appDataFolder
        In android system, it is recommend to set the appDataFolder to getApplicationContext().getFilesDir().getAbsolutePath().
        If this is not set, it will use getApplicationContext().getFilesDir().getAbsolutePath() in Android and "./" in PC Java.

        protected ProcessFactoryDefault processFactory

    • Method Detail

      • getCurrentCom

        public static java.lang.String getCurrentCom()
        To get the current comm type.

      • SetCommSetting

        public boolean SetCommSetting(CommSetting comm)
        Set the communication configuration
        See Also:
        CommSetting

      • SetLogSetting

        public void SetLogSetting(SubLogSetting subLogSetting)
        Set the SubLogSetting

        If the SubLogSetting is set in the current POSLink instance, then the Log recorded during the execution of the POSLink will adopt the configuration of SubLogSetting.
        Parameters:
        subLogSetting - SubLogSetting

      • GetCommSetting

        public CommSetting GetCommSetting()
        Get the communication configuration
        See Also:
        CommSetting

      • CancelTrans

        public void CancelTrans()
        To cancel last process by sending command to terminal. Even if this called, you still wait until the ProcessTrans() finishes.

        Call POSLink to cancel transaction. Currently it’s available for terminating transaction commands only. When perform the transaction requests, the Cancel is only effective before the transaction is calling communication module to transmit data between terminal and host.

      • GetReportedStatus

        public int GetReportedStatus()
        Get the terminal reported integer status.

        The POSLink will report the status during the payment processing. The POS will get following status report while waiting for the authorization response
        0 Off (Default)
        1 On
        Returned values:

        -1: No status returned.
        0: Ready for card input.
        1: Ready for PIN entry
        2: Ready for Signature
        3: Ready for Online Processing.
        4: Ready for second card input (for REPLACE/MERGE).
        5: Ready for Signature retry by pressing CLEAR key
        6: Ready for PIN retry by inputting wrong offline PIN for EMV

        For INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV:
        9000000: Ready for card input. (Same as 0)
        9000001: Ready for PIN entry. (Same as 1)
        9000002: Ready for Signature. (Same as 2)
        9000003: Ready for Online Processing. (Same as 3)
        9000004: Ready for second card input (For REPLACE/MERGE). (Same as 4)
        9000005: Ready for Signature retry by pressing CLEAR key. (Same as 5)
        9000006: Ready for PIN retry by inputting wrong offline PIN for EMV. (Same as 6)
        9000007: Please See Phone
        9000008: Please Try Another Card
        9000009: Please Present One Card Only
        9000010: Fallback Swipe
        9020002: Ready for entering cashback.
        9040003: Remove Card
        9040010: Re-Insert Card

        Only valid when tag is turned on.
        Returns:
        the terminal reported integer status

      • setReportStatusListener

        public void setReportStatusListener(AbstractPosLink.ReportStatusListener listener)
        Register a callback to be invoked when terminal reported integer status.
        Parameters:
        listener - ReportStatusListener
        Since:
        V1.08.00

      • ProcessTrans

        public ProcessTransResult ProcessTrans()

        Send message according to the input objects ,and the receive response from POS terminal and unpack it,then create the corresponding response object. User should get the response items from PaymentResponse,anageResponse,BatchResponse or ReportResponse object.Notes the response object is only valid while the ProcessTransResult.Code is OK

        This method will start connection with POS. It is must to put it in a background thread and wait for its result. If it is run in the main thread it will return an error: Cannot Run in Main Thread.

        Returns:
        the ProcessTransResult object with specify result
        See Also:
        ProcessTransResult

      • getProtocolType

        public boolean getProtocolType()

      • ProcessTrans

        public ProcessTransResult ProcessTrans(java.lang.String request)
        Send low-level messages and return the Response from POS terminal. The Response is in ProcessTransResult.Response.

        This method will start connection with POS. It is must to put it in a background thread and wait for its result. If it is run in the main thread it will return an error: Cannot Run in Main Thread.

        Parameters:
        request - low-level message
        Returns:
        the ProcessTransResult object with specify result
        See Also:
        ProcessTransResult

      • ProcessTrans

        public ProcessTransResult ProcessTrans(java.util.List<java.lang.String> requests)
        Send low-level messages and return the list of Response from POS terminal. The list of Response is in ProcessTransResult.Responses.

        This method will start connection with POS. It is must to put it in a background thread and wait for its result. If it is run in the main thread it will return an error: Cannot Run in Main Thread.

        Parameters:
        requests - the list of low-level message
        Returns:
        the ProcessTransResult object with specify result
        See Also:
        ProcessTransResult

      • isProcessing

        public static boolean isProcessing()
        Returns:
        whether ProcessTrans() running

      • afterProcessTrans

        protected abstract void afterProcessTrans()

      • beforeProcessTrans

        protected abstract void beforeProcessTrans()

      • unpackTrans

        protected void unpackTrans()

      • filterResponse

        protected boolean filterResponse(java.lang.String tranType)

      • uploadLog

        public UploadResult uploadLog(java.lang.String[] filePaths)
        Call POSLink to tell terminal to upload terminal logs and then POSLink upload POSLink log itself. Terminal logs and POSLink logs will be uploaded to the PAX server.

        Need to set CommSetting to communicate with the terminal. Upload of POSLink's own log needs to rely on 'jsch-0.1.55.jar'.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Note:
        1.When terminal upload log, the log which is uploaded successful will be delete from terminal.
        2.The terminal log is saved in only one log file, won’t be split into many files by date, so whenever you use upload log command the terminal will only upload one file.
        3.Only some necessary information or exceptions occur or errors occur, the log will be generated.

        The disclaimers: https://www.pax.us/privacy-policy/

        Parameters:
        filePaths - String[] logFileNames: The log names including path. If logFileNames is null or empty, POSLink will upload all txt files from folder whose path is set in LogManageMent and if folder path. If you want files of your choice, please add log names including path to logFileNames. The upload logs file should less then 30mb before compress.
        Example:
        logFileNames[0] = “.../PAX/POSLog20200708.log”;
        logFileNames[1] = “.../PAX/POSLog20200709.log”;
        Returns:
        UploadResult

      • getServerIP

        public java.lang.String getServerIP(java.lang.String host)
        Get IP from host name
        Parameters:
        host - host name
        Returns:

      • getVersion

        public static java.lang.String getVersion()

        Returns:
        POSLink SDK version.
